{{Infobox technique
| name = Mirror of Silence
| introduced = The Captain
}}

''See also: [[Mirror of Silence Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Mirror of Silence is a highly sophisticated and proprietary spell belonging to the [[Vallenar Corporation]]. Regarded as a masterpiece of magic, it allows a caster to manifest temporary copies of tangible assets or targets by drawing from the [[Aether]] to bring potential possibilities into reality. This ability can be used to multiply resources, such as ships, fighters, weapons, or even people, providing a significant tactical advantage on the battlefield. While basic applications produce colorless, black-and-white shadows, the most advanced uses of the spell can create clones that are indistinguishable from the originals. The magic is complex enough to be integrated into technology like missiles and can be expanded to a massive scale, allowing a caster to monitor vast areas of the galaxy or manage numerous conflicts simultaneously. However, the spell is temporary and requires intense concentration to maintain; once the incantation is ended or the magic is recalled, all conjured copies vanish.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The Mirror of Silence is a masterpiece [[Archmage]] spell owned by the [[Vallenar Corporation]] that can be sealed into an object. This complex technique functions by bringing shadows of potential to [[life]], allowing for the multiplication of resources or the creation of indistinguishable copies of people and missiles. [[Benri Vallenar]] has utilized the spell to produce clones of himself, while it has also been used to clone Legionnaires and create black-and-white copies of casters to deploy sealing spells. Although the spell can be used to watch memories, its various copies eventually fade away.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is a complex spell that the protagonist can seal into an object.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11">''The Captain'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is a proprietary spell of the [[Vallenar Corporation]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] causes missiles to sprout black-and-white clones of themselves.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]] uses the [[Mirror of Silence]] to create indistinguishable copies of himself.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c20">''The Captain'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] can create copies of people.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c25">''The Captain'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell used to make copies.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27">''The Captain'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is an [[Archmage]] spell considered a masterpiece.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] brings shadows of potential to [[life]] to multiply resources.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] copies have faded.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32">''The Captain'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] can be used to watch memories.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34">''The Captain'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is used to clone Legionnaires.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]] uses the [[Mirror of Silence]] technique.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is an entity or object that was not seen during the battle.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Mirror of Silence]] creates a black-and-white copy of the caster to send out sealing spells.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46">''The Captain'', chapter 46</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell that can be used to identify flaws in construction.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell that can be chanted.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell used by the narrator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell that can create a single clone.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mirror of Silence]] can be used to create clones of a fabricator.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is used to create long-term duplicates.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c21">''The Knight'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Mirror of Silence]] creates copies constructed from the [[Aether]] based on possibilities.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Mirror of Silence is an information security spell and an artifact owned by [[Benri]], whose successful demonstration of the magic served as part of his initiation into [[Solstice]]. This enchantment allows a Lightcaster to clone shots as they are fired, though these copies are short-lived and last approximately five seconds before dissolving into nothing. The spell can also cause [[Sola]] to split into six, resulting in clones that eventually vanish. [[Benri]] later enhanced the magic by combining his artifact with [[Teranon]]'s analytical spells to trace various possibilities, a process involving a clone that serves as a component of the new [[Iron King]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is an information security spell.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c5">''The Pilot'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Proving the [[Mirror of Silence]] would work was part of [[Benri]]'s initiation into [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c11">''The Pilot'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mirror of Silence]] is a spell that causes [[Sola]] to split into six.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] causes clones to vanish.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is an enchantment that allows a Lightcaster to copy shots.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] copy can be dissolved to nothing.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] is an artifact owned by [[Benri]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38">''The Pilot'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] clone is a component of the new [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri]]'s enhanced spell combines [[Teranon]]'s analytical spells with his own [[Mirror of Silence]] to trace possibilities.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] clones shots as they are fired.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Mirror of Silence]] spell used by the narrator creates short-lived copies that last about five seconds.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c44">''The Pilot'', chapter 44</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“Mirror of Silence,” I finished. Sola split into six.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Techniques]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
